---
title: "Assignment 9 Answer Key"
author: "Ellen Bledsoe"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

### 1. Working with Raster Data in `terra` (20 pts)


```{r answers-1}
# 1a
# When loading terra library, will print version number to console
library(terra)

# 1b
# Read in data with rast
prec <- rast("image_files/global_precipitation.tif")
# Print prec raster to see EPSG (4326)
prec

# 1c
# Geographic extent of Himalayas, roughly, is (lat, lon)
# Top left: 40.37, 69.33
# Bottom right: 26.93, 97.15
# ext takes arguments in this order: xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax
# or, in human: left, right, bottom, top
hima_ext <- ext(c(69.33, 97.15, 26.93, 40.37))

# 1d 
# Crop precipitation to Himalayan extent
prec_hima <- crop(prec, hima_ext)
# Print new raster to see extent
prec_hima
```

### 2. Printing Maps (20 points)


```{r answers-2}
# 2a
# Plot the cropped precipitation data
plot(prec_hima)

# 2b
# Plot the same data, this time adding a title
plot(prec_hima, main = "Mean annual precipitation (mm)")

# 2c
# Define the palette first, using the topo.colors function
prec_cols <- topo.colors(n = 50)
# Plot the map with those new colors
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)

# 2d
# Define palette with hcl.colors, several options from Color Brewer, example 
# below uses Yellow-Green-Blue palette, reversed so blue is highest
prec_cols <- rev(hcl.colors(n = 50, palette = "YlGnBu"))
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)
```

### 3. Working with Vector Data in `terra` (20 points)


```{r answer-3}
# 3a
# Create data frame for three peaks
peaks <- data.frame(peak = c("K2", "Annapurna", "Mount Everest"),
                    lat = c(35.93, 28.53, 28.05),
                    lon = c(76.50, 83.81, 86.91))

# 3b
# Convert data frame to SpatVector object
peaks_vect <- vect(peaks)

# 3c
# Draw base precipitation plot
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)
plot(peaks_vect, add = TRUE)

# 3d
# Add lines with lines function
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)
plot(peaks_vect, add = TRUE)
text(peaks_vect, labels = "peak", pos = 4)
```

### 4. Modifying Raster Values (20 points)


```{r answer-4}
# 4a
# Load the data
elev <- rast("image_files/global_elevation.tif")
# Print the object to see range of elevations, safe to infer units are meters
elev

# 4b
# 1 foot = 3.28084 meters
elev <- elev * 3.28084

# 4c
# Crop to same geographic extent as for precipitation data
elev_hima <- crop(elev, hima_ext)

# 4d
plot(elev_hima)
```

### 5. Converting Raster Objects to Spatial Vector Objects (20 points)

```{r answer-5}
# 5a
# Define elevation threshold
alpine_min <- 13000

# 5b
# Create logical raster of high elevation (TRUE/FALSE)
elev_alpine <- elev_hima >= alpine_min

# 5c
# Convert all TRUE values to NA (want the polygon to shade out areas of low 
# elevation, which are FALSE in this case)
elev_alpine[elev_alpine] <- NA

# 5d
# Convert the raster with FALSE values only at places below 13,000 feet
low_elev_poly <- as.polygons(elev_alpine)

# 5e
# Plot the precipitation map
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)
# Add black polygon with 50% transparency to shade out low-elevation sites
polys(x = low_elev_poly, col = "black", border = NA, alpha = 0.5)
```

---
title: 'Week 2: Assignment Answer Key'
author: "Ellen Bledsoe"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format: pdf
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

1.  Basic Expressions

```{r}
2 - 10
3 * 5
9 / 2
5 - 3 * 2
(5 - 3) * 2
4 ** 2
8 / 2 ** 2
```

2.  Basic Variables

```{r}
mass_lbs <- 3.5
mass_kg <- mass_lbs / 2.2046
mass_kg
```

3.  More Variables

```{r}
grams <- 250
number <- 3
biomass <- grams * number
biomass_kg <- biomass / 1000
print(biomass_kg)
```

4.  Built-In Functions

```{r}
abs(-15.5)

round(4.483847, 1)

round(3.8, 0)

toupper("species")

tolower("SPECIES")

value <- sqrt(2.6)
value <- round(value, 2)
value

round(sqrt(2.6), 2)
```

5.  Modify the Code

```{r}
# grams of carbon per sq. meter
site1_g_carbon_m2_day <- 5
site2_g_carbon_m2_day <- 2.3

# number of sq. meters
site1_area_m2 <- 200
site2_area_m2 <- 450

# daily NPP per site
site1_npp_day <- site1_g_carbon_m2_day * site1_area_m2
site2_npp_day <- site2_g_carbon_m2_day * site2_area_m2

# a.  The sum of the total daily NPP for the two sites combined.
total_npp_day <- site1_npp_day + site2_npp_day
total_npp_day

# b.  The difference between the total daily NPP for the two sites.
daily_diff <- abs(site1_npp_day - site2_npp_day)
daily_diff

# c.  The total NPP over a year for the two sites combined.
total_npp_year <- total_npp_day * 365
total_npp_year
```

6.  Basic Vectors

```{r}
numbers <- c(5, 2, 26, 8, 16)

length(numbers)

numbers[3]

min(numbers)

max(numbers)

mean(numbers)

numbers[1:3]

sum(numbers)
```

7.  Null Vectors

```{r}
numbers <- c(7, 6, 22, 5, NA, 42)

min(numbers, na.rm = TRUE)
max(numbers, na.rm = TRUE)
mean(numbers, na.rm = TRUE)
sum(numbers, na.rm = TRUE)
```

8.  Shrub Volume Vectors

```{r}
length <- c(2.2, 2.1, 2.7, 3.0, 3.1, 2.5, 1.9, 1.1, 3.5, 2.9)
width <- c(1.3, 2.2, 1.5, 4.5, 3.1, NA, 1.8, 0.5, 2.0, 2.7)
height <- c(9.6, 7.6, 2.2, 1.5, 4.0, 3.0, 4.5, 2.3, 7.5, 3.2)

#1
volume <- length * width * height
volume

#2
sum(volume, na.rm = TRUE)

#3

height[length > 2.5]

#4
height[height > 5]

#5
height[1:5]

#6
volume[1:3]

print("Challenge")
num_shrubs <- length(volume)
volume[(num_shrubs - 4):num_shrubs]
```

```{r}
numbers[1:3]

sum(numbers)
```

7.  Null Vectors

```{r}
numbers <- c(7, 6, 22, 5, NA, 42)

min(numbers, na.rm = TRUE)
max(numbers, na.rm = TRUE)
mean(numbers, na.rm = TRUE)
sum(numbers, na.rm = TRUE)
```

8.  Shrub Volume Vectors

```{r}
length <- c(2.2, 2.1, 2.7, 3.0, 3.1, 2.5, 1.9, 1.1, 3.5, 2.9)
width <- c(1.3, 2.2, 1.5, 4.5, 3.1, NA, 1.8, 0.5, 2.0, 2.7)
height <- c(9.6, 7.6, 2.2, 1.5, 4.0, 3.0, 4.5, 2.3, 7.5, 3.2)

#1

volume

#2
sum(volume, na.rm = TRUE)

#3

height[length > 2.5]

#4
height[height > 5]

#5
height[1:5]

#6
volume[1:3]

print("Challenge")
num_shrubs <- length(volume)
volume[(num_shrubs - 4):num_shrubs]
```

---
title: "Week 4 Assignment"
author: "Ellen Bledsoe"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format: pdf
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```


```{r message=FALSE}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r message=FALSE}
surveys <- read_csv("surveys.csv")
species <- read_csv("species.csv")
plots <- read_csv("plots.csv")
```

### 2. Portal Data Aggregation (15 pts)

```{r}

# 1. Use the group_by() and summarize() functions to get a count of the number of individuals in each species ID.
print("2a")
surveys %>%
  group_by(species_id) %>%
  summarize(count = n())


# 2. Use the group_by() and summarize() functions to get a count of the number of individuals in each species ID in each year.
print("2b")
surveys %>%
  group_by(species_id, year) %>%
  summarize(count = n())

# 3. Use the filter(), group_by(), and summarize() functions to get the mean mass of species DO in each year.
 print("2c")
 surveys %>%
  filter(species_id == "DO") %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(avg_mass = mean(weight, na.rm = TRUE))

```

### 3. Shrub Volume Aggregation (15 pts)


```{r message=FALSE}
shrub_dims <- read_csv('shrub-volume-data.csv')
print("3a")
shrub_dims %>%
  group_by(experiment) %>%
  summarize(avg_height = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE))

print("3b")
shrub_dims %>%
  group_by(experiment) %>%
  summarize(avg_height = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_height = max(height, na.rm = TRUE))

print("3c")
shrub_dims %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  summarize(avg_height = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_height = max(height, na.rm = TRUE),
            min_height = min(height, na.rm = TRUE))
```

### 4. Portal Data Joins (25 pts)

```{r}
# 1.
print("4a")
inner_join(surveys, species, by = "species_id")

# 2.
print("4b")
inner_join(surveys, species, by = "species_id") %>%
inner_join(., plots, by = "plot_id")

# 3.
print("4c")
surveys %>%
  inner_join(plots, by = "plot_id") %>%
  filter(plot_type == "Control")
```

### 5. Portal Data `dplyr` Review (25 pts)

```{r}
print("5a")
surveys %>%
  inner_join(species, by = "species_id") %>%
  inner_join(plots, by = "plot_id") %>%
  filter(plot_type == "Control" | plot_type == "Long-term Krat Exclosure") %>%
  filter(taxa == "Rodent") %>%
  select('year', 'genus', 'species', 'weight', 'plot_type') %>%
  filter(!is.na(weight))

print("5b")
surveys %>%
  inner_join(species, by = "species_id") %>%
  inner_join(plots, by = "plot_id") %>%
  filter(plot_type == "Control" | plot_type == "Long-term Krat Exclosure",
         taxa == "Rodent",
         !is.na(weight)) %>%
  select('year', 'genus', 'species', 'weight', 'plot_type') %>%
  group_by(year, plot_type) |>
  summarize(min_weight = min(weight),
            max_weight = max(weight),
            mean_weight = mean(weight))
```


### 6. Shrub Volume Bind (10 pts)


```{r}
new_data <- data.frame(respiratory_rate = c(2.2, 4.0, 6.1, 2.3, 4.1, 6.2, 1.8, 3.5, 5.7, 1.9, 3.5, 5.8, 2.0, 4.7, 6.2),
                       average_temp_C = c(15.1, 20.2, 24.7, 15.2, 22.0, 25.1, 14.2, 19.0, 23.6, 14.9, 20.3, 24.1, 19.2, 22.7, 25.0))

```

```{r}
bind_cols(shrub_dims, new_data)
```

### 7. Shrub Volume Join (15 pts)

```{r message=FALSE}
experiments <- read_csv("https://datacarpentry.org/semester-biology/data/shrub-volume-experiments.csv")
sites <- read_csv("https://datacarpentry.org/semester-biology/data/shrub-volume-sites.csv")
```


```{r}

# 1. Import the experiments data and then use `inner_join` to combine
print("7a")
inner_join(shrub_dims, experiments, by = "experiment")

# 2. Import the sites data and the combine it with both the data on shrub
# dimensions and the data on experiments to produce a single data frame
# that contains all of the data.
print("7b")
shrub_dims_experiments <- inner_join(shrub_dims, experiments, by = "experiment")
shrub_data <- inner_join(shrub_dims_experiments, sites, by = "site")
shrub_data
```

---
title: "Week 5 Assignment"
author: "Ellen Bledsoe"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format: pdf
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

### 1. Acacia and Ants (20 pts)

```{r}
acacia <- read_tsv("ACACIA_DREPANOLOBIUM_SURVEY.txt", na = c("", "dead"))
```

```{r}
# 1a

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter")

# 1b

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter")

# 1c

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1, color = ANT)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter")

# 1d

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter") +
  facet_wrap(~ANT)

# 1e

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter") +
  facet_wrap(~ANT) +
  geom_smooth()
```

### 2. Mass vs. Metabolism (20 pts)

```{r}
size_mr_data <- data.frame(
  body_mass = c(32000, 37800, 347000, 4200, 196500, 100000,
    4290, 32000, 65000, 69125, 9600, 133300, 150000, 407000,
    115000, 67000,325000, 21500, 58588, 65320, 85000, 135000,
    20500, 1613, 1618),
  metabolic_rate = c(49.984, 51.981, 306.770, 10.075, 230.073,
    148.949, 11.966, 46.414, 123.287, 106.663, 20.619, 180.150,
    200.830, 224.779, 148.940, 112.430, 286.847, 46.347,
    142.863, 106.670, 119.660, 104.150, 33.165, 4.900, 4.865),
  family = c("Antilocapridae", "Antilocapridae", "Bovidae",
    "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae",
    "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae",
    "Camelidae", "Camelidae", "Canidae", "Cervidae",
    "Cervidae", "Cervidae", "Cervidae", "Cervidae", "Suidae",
    "Tayassuidae", "Tragulidae", "Tragulidae"))
```

```{r}
# 1.  A graph of body mass vs. metabolic rate
ggplot(size_mr_data, aes(body_mass, metabolic_rate)) + geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Body Mass", y = "Metabolic Rate")

# 2.  A graph of body mass vs. metabolic rate, log scaled, with pt size 5.
ggplot(data = size_mr_data, mapping = aes(x = body_mass, y = metabolic_rate)) +
  geom_point(size=3) +
  labs(x = "Body Mass", y = "Metabolic Rate") +
  scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10()

# 3. The same plot as (2), but with the different families indicated using color.
ggplot(size_mr_data, aes(x = body_mass, y = metabolic_rate, color = family)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Body Mass", y = "Metabolic Rate")

# 4. The same plot as (2), but with the different families each in their own subplot.
ggplot(size_mr_data, aes(x = body_mass, y = metabolic_rate)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~family) +
  labs(x = "Body Mass", y = "Metabolic Rate")
```

### 3. Acacia and Ants Histograms (20 pts)

```{r}
# 1

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = ANT)) +
  geom_bar()

# 2

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = HEIGHT)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  labs(x = "Height(m)", y = "Number of Acacia")

# 3

ggplot(data = acacia) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = AXIS1), fill = 'red', alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = AXIS2), fill = 'black', alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(x = "Canopy Diameter", y = "Number of Acacia")

# 4

ggplot(data = acacia) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = AXIS1), fill = 'red', alpha = 0.3, bins = 10) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = AXIS2), fill = 'black', alpha = 0.3, bins = 10) +
  labs(x = "Canopy Diameter", y = "Number of Acacia") +
  facet_wrap(~TREATMENT)
```

### 4. Acacia and Ants Data Manipulation (20 pts)

```{r}
trees <- read_tsv("TREE.txt",
                  col_types = list(HEIGHT = col_double(),
                                   AXIS_2 = col_double())) |> drop_na(TREATMENT)
```

```{r}
# 4a
print("4a")
trees <- mutate(trees, canopy_area = AXIS_1 * AXIS_2)
trees

# 4b
print("4b")
select(trees, SURVEY, YEAR, SITE, SPECIES, canopy_area)

# 4c
print("4c")
ggplot(data = trees, mapping = aes(x = canopy_area, y = HEIGHT, color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(x = "Canopy Area (m)", y = "Height (m)") +
  facet_wrap(~SPECIES)

# 4d
print("4d")
trees <- filter(trees, AXIS_1 < 20, AXIS_2 < 20)
ggplot(data = trees, mapping = aes(x = canopy_area, y = HEIGHT, color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(x = "Canopy Area (m)", y = "Height (m)") +
  facet_wrap(~SPECIES)

# 4e
print("4e")
abundance_time <- trees %>%
  group_by(YEAR, SPECIES) %>%
  summarize(abundance = n())
abundance_time

# 4f
print("4f")
ggplot(data = abundance_time, mapping = aes(x = YEAR, y = abundance)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~SPECIES, scales = "free_y")
```

### 5. Adult vs. Newborn Size (20 pts)

```{r}
mammal_histories <- read_tsv("Mammal_lifehistories_v2.txt",
                             na = c("-999", "-999.00")) %>%
  rename(mass_g = `mass(g)`,
         gestation_mo = `gestation(mo)`,
         newborn_g = `newborn(g)`,
         weaning_mo = `weaning(mo)`,
         wean_mass_g = `wean mass(g)`,
         AFR_mo = `AFR(mo)`,
         max_life_mo = `max. life(mo)`,
         litter_size = `litter size`,
         litters_per_year = `litters/year`)
```

```{r}

# a. Graph adult mass vs. newborn mass.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")

# b. Graph adult mass vs. newborn mass, with both axes scaled logarithmically.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")

# c. Graph adult mass vs. newborn mass, log-scaled, with data colored by order.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=order)) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")

# d. Use `facet_wrap` to create subplot for each order.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~ order) +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")

# e. use `geom_smooth` to fit a linear model to each order.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~ order) +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")
```

### 7. Graphing Data from Multiple Tables (*Optional*)

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = trees, aes(x = CIRC, y = HEIGHT), color = "gray") +
  geom_smooth(data = trees, aes(x = CIRC, y = HEIGHT), color = "black", method = "lm") +
  geom_point(data = acacia, aes(x = CIRC, y = HEIGHT), color = "red") +
  geom_smooth(data = acacia, aes(x = CIRC, y = HEIGHT), color = "red", method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~ TREATMENT) +
  labs(x = "Circumfrence (cm)",
       y = "Height (m)") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_light()
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

### 1. Acacia and Ants (20 pts)

```{r}
acacia <- read_tsv("ACACIA_DREPANOLOBIUM_SURVEY.txt", na = c("", "dead"))
```

```{r}
# 1a

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter")

# 1b

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter")

# 1c

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1, color = ANT)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter")

# 1d

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter") +
  facet_wrap(~ANT)

# 1e

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = CIRC, y = AXIS1)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Circumference", y = "Canopy Diameter") +
  facet_wrap(~ANT) +
  geom_smooth()
```

### 2. Mass vs. Metabolism (20 pts)

```{r}
size_mr_data <- data.frame(
  body_mass = c(32000, 37800, 347000, 4200, 196500, 100000,
    4290, 32000, 65000, 69125, 9600, 133300, 150000, 407000,
    115000, 67000,325000, 21500, 58588, 65320, 85000, 135000,
    20500, 1613, 1618),
  metabolic_rate = c(49.984, 51.981, 306.770, 10.075, 230.073,
    148.949, 11.966, 46.414, 123.287, 106.663, 20.619, 180.150,
    200.830, 224.779, 148.940, 112.430, 286.847, 46.347,
    142.863, 106.670, 119.660, 104.150, 33.165, 4.900, 4.865),
  family = c("Antilocapridae", "Antilocapridae", "Bovidae",
    "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae",
    "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae", "Bovidae",
    "Camelidae", "Camelidae", "Canidae", "Cervidae",
    "Cervidae", "Cervidae", "Cervidae", "Cervidae", "Suidae",
    "Tayassuidae", "Tragulidae", "Tragulidae"))
```

```{r}
# 1.  A graph of body mass vs. metabolic rate
ggplot(size_mr_data, aes(body_mass, metabolic_rate)) + geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Body Mass", y = "Metabolic Rate")

# 2.  A graph of body mass vs. metabolic rate, log scaled, with pt size 5.
ggplot(data = size_mr_data, mapping = aes(x = body_mass, y = metabolic_rate)) +
  geom_point(size=3) +
  labs(x = "Body Mass", y = "Metabolic Rate") +
  scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10()

# 3. The same plot as (2), but with the different families indicated using color.
ggplot(size_mr_data, aes(x = body_mass, y = metabolic_rate, color = family)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Body Mass", y = "Metabolic Rate")

# 4. The same plot as (2), but with the different families each in their own subplot.
ggplot(size_mr_data, aes(x = body_mass, y = metabolic_rate)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~family) +
  labs(x = "Body Mass", y = "Metabolic Rate")
```

### 3. Acacia and Ants Histograms (20 pts)

```{r}
# 1

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = ANT)) +
  geom_bar()

# 2

ggplot(data = acacia, mapping = aes(x = HEIGHT)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  labs(x = "Height(m)", y = "Number of Acacia")

# 3

ggplot(data = acacia) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = AXIS1), fill = 'red', alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = AXIS2), fill = 'black', alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(x = "Canopy Diameter", y = "Number of Acacia")

# 4

ggplot(data = acacia) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = AXIS1), fill = 'red', alpha = 0.3, bins = 10) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = AXIS2), fill = 'black', alpha = 0.3, bins = 10) +
  labs(x = "Canopy Diameter", y = "Number of Acacia") +
  facet_wrap(~TREATMENT)
```

### 4. Acacia and Ants Data Manipulation (20 pts)

```{r}
trees <- read_tsv("TREE.txt",
                  col_types = list(HEIGHT = col_double(),
                                   AXIS_2 = col_double())) |> drop_na(TREATMENT)
```

```{r}
# 4a
print("4a")
trees <- mutate(trees, canopy_area = AXIS_1 * AXIS_2)
trees

# 4b
print("4b")
select(trees, SURVEY, YEAR, SITE, SPECIES, canopy_area)

# 4c
print("4c")
ggplot(data = trees, mapping = aes(x = canopy_area, y = HEIGHT, color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(x = "Canopy Area (m)", y = "Height (m)") +
  facet_wrap(~SPECIES)

# 4d
print("4d")
trees <- filter(trees, AXIS_1 < 20, AXIS_2 < 20)
ggplot(data = trees, mapping = aes(x = canopy_area, y = HEIGHT, color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(x = "Canopy Area (m)", y = "Height (m)") +
  facet_wrap(~SPECIES)

# 4e
print("4e")
abundance_time <- trees %>%
  group_by(YEAR, SPECIES) %>%
  summarize(abundance = n())
abundance_time

# 4f
print("4f")
ggplot(data = abundance_time, mapping = aes(x = YEAR, y = abundance)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~SPECIES, scales = "free_y")
```

### 5. Adult vs. Newborn Size (20 pts)

```{r}
mammal_histories <- read_tsv("Mammal_lifehistories_v2.txt",
                             na = c("-999", "-999.00")) %>%
  rename(mass_g = `mass(g)`,
         gestation_mo = `gestation(mo)`,
         newborn_g = `newborn(g)`,
         weaning_mo = `weaning(mo)`,
         wean_mass_g = `wean mass(g)`,
         AFR_mo = `AFR(mo)`,
         max_life_mo = `max. life(mo)`,
         litter_size = `litter size`,
         litters_per_year = `litters/year`)
```

```{r}

# a. Graph adult mass vs. newborn mass.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")

# b. Graph adult mass vs. newborn mass, with both axes scaled logarithmically.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")

# c. Graph adult mass vs. newborn mass, log-scaled, with data colored by order.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=order)) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")

# d. Use `facet_wrap` to create subplot for each order.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~ order) +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")

# e. use `geom_smooth` to fit a linear model to each order.
ggplot(mammal_histories, aes(x = mass_g, y = newborn_g)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~ order) +
  labs(x = "Adult Mass", y = "Newborn Mass")
```

### 7. Graphing Data from Multiple Tables (*Optional*)

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = trees, aes(x = CIRC, y = HEIGHT), color = "gray") +
  geom_smooth(data = trees, aes(x = CIRC, y = HEIGHT), color = "black", method = "lm") +
  geom_point(data = acacia, aes(x = CIRC, y = HEIGHT), color = "red") +
  geom_smooth(data = acacia, aes(x = CIRC, y = HEIGHT), color = "red", method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~ TREATMENT) +
  labs(x = "Circumfrence (cm)",
       y = "Height (m)") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_light()
```

title: "Week 6 Assignment"

format: pdf

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

# Week 6 Assignment

# Assignment Exercises

### Set-up

Load the packages we will need. You can either load all of them individually (`readr`, `dplyr`, `tidyr`, `ggplot2`) or load the `tidyverse` package.

```{r message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```

### 1. Forest Area per Country (15 pts)


```{r message=FALSE}
forest <- read_csv("forest_per_country.csv", skip = 4, col_names = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# a
forest <- forest %>%
  pivot_longer(`1990`:`2022`, names_to = "Year", values_to = "ForestArea_sqkm")
forest

# b.
forest <- forest %>%
  drop_na(ForestArea_sqkm)
forest

# c
ggplot(forest, aes(Year, ForestArea_sqkm)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "forestgreen") +
  labs(x = "Year",
       y = "Forest Area (sq. km)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 0.5))
```

### 2. OECD Data (10 pts)


```{r message=FALSE}
print("2a")
oecd <- read_csv("oecd_annual_data.csv", skip = 4, col_names = TRUE)
oecd
```

```{r}
print("2b: last 6 rows")
oecd <- oecd %>%
  fill(OECD_member)
tail(oecd)

print("2c")
oecd <- oecd %>%
  pivot_longer(`2000`:`2022`, names_to = "Year", values_to = "MarineProtectedArea_sqkm")
oecd

print("2d")
oecd |>
  filter(OECD_member == "Non-OECD Economies") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Country, values_from = MarineProtectedArea_sqkm) |>
  select(-OECD_member)
```

### 3. Santa Cruz Rodents Data Cleaning (20 pts)


```{r message=FALSE}
rodents <- read_csv("capture_data.csv")

#a
rodents <- rodents %>%
  rename(TrapID = `Trap ID`, Status = `Status (R/N)`, TotalWeight = `Total Weight`,
         BagWeight = `Bag weight`, AnimalWeight = `Animal Weight`,
         HindfoodLength = `Hind foot length`, TailLength = `Tail length`,
         HairSample = `Hair sample (Y/N)`, Position = `Position (R/L)`)

#b
print("3b")
rodents <- rodents %>%
  fill(Site)
rodents

#c
rodents <- rodents %>%
  mutate(Species = replace(Species, Species == "SIOC?", "SIOC"),
         Species = replace(Species, Species == "DIME?", "DIME"))

#d
rodents <- rodents %>%
  mutate(TailLength = replace(TailLength, TailLength == "~15.5", "15.5"))

#e
rodents <- rodents %>%
  mutate(HairSample = na_if(HairSample, "?"),
         Position = na_if(Position, "?"))

#f
print("3f")
ggplot(rodents, aes(TotalWeight, fill = Site)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.5, position = "identity", binwidth = 10) +
  facet_wrap(~ Species) +
  labs(x = "Weight (g)", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_classic()

```


### 4. Remembering Joins (15 pts)

```{r message=FALSE}
veg <- read_csv("microsite_grouped_veg.csv")

#a
veg <- veg %>%
  rename(RecordID = `...1`, TrapID = `Trap Location`, DistancetoVeg_m = `Distance to Vegetation (m)`,
         VegetationType = `Type of Vegetation`, PercentCover = `Percent Veg Cover`, DistancetoWater_m = `Distance to Water (m)`)

#b
print("4b")
veg <- veg %>%
  select(Site, TrapID, Grouped_Veg)
veg

#c
print("4c")
inner_join(rodents, veg)
```

### 5. Santa Cruz Rodents Wrangling (20 pts)

```{r}
#a
print("5a")
rodents <- rodents %>%
  separate(Date, into = c("Year", "Month", "Day"), sep = "-")
rodents

#b
print("5b")
rodents <- rodents %>%
  unite("Date", Year:Day, sep = "-")
rodents

#c
print("5c")
sp_by_site <- rodents %>%
  group_by(Site, Species) %>%
  summarize(Count = n())
sp_by_site

#d
print("5d")
sp_by_site %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Species,
              values_from = Count,
              values_fill = 0)
```

### 6. Mammals (20 pts)

The code chunk below has some made-up mammal data. Run the code chunk below to complete question 5.

```{r}
mammals <- data.frame(site = c(1,1,2,3,3,3),
                      taxon = c('Suncus etruscus', 'Sorex cinereus',
                                'Myotis nigricans', 'Notiosorex crawfordi',
                                'Suncus etruscus', 'Myotis nigricans'),
                      density = c(6.2, 5.2, 11.0, 1.2, 9.4, 9.6),
                      avg_mass = c(4.2, 5, 9.1, 8.6, 4.1, 8.7))
```

```{r}
print("6a")
mammals <- mammals %>%
  separate(taxon, c("genus", "species"), sep = " ")
mammals

print("6b")
mammals <- mammals %>%
  pivot_longer(density:avg_mass, names_to = "measurement", values_to = "value")
mammals

print("6c")
mammals <- mammals %>%
  unite("taxon", genus, species, sep = " ")
mammals

print("6d")
mammals <- mammals %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = measurement, values_from = value)
mammals
```

---
title: "Assignment 14"
author: "Ellen Bledsoe"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format: pdf
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

```{r message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```

### 2. When Did You Knit This Document? (5 pts)

```{r}
#a
today()

#b
now()
```

### 3. Plant Vouchers (20 pts)

```{r}
vouchers <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weecology/DNA_metabarcoding/master/data/collection_data/plant_voucher_collection.csv")
```

```{r}
#a
vouchers <- vouchers %>%
  mutate(collection_date = make_date(year = year,
                                     month = month,
                                     day = day), .after = day)
vouchers

#b
min(vouchers$collection_date)

#c
max(vouchers$collection_date)

#d
as.duration(interval(min(vouchers$collection_date), max(vouchers$collection_date)))

#e
vouchers %>%
  mutate(DOY = yday(collection_date), .after = collection_date)
```

### 4. NDVI from the Santa Rita Experimental Range (20 pts)

```{r}
phenocam <- read_csv("neon_srer_ndvi_phencom.csv", skip = 17, col_names = TRUE,
                    col_types = "ccncc") %>%
  select(date, local_std_time, contains("mean"), NDVI_c)
```

```{r}
#a create datetime column using unite
phenocam <- phenocam %>%
  unite("datetime", date, local_std_time, sep = " ")
phenocam

#b convert to POSIXct using lubridate
phenocam <- phenocam %>%
  mutate(datetime = ymd_hms(datetime))
phenocam

#c duration of dataset
as.duration(interval(min(phenocam$datetime), max(phenocam$datetime)))

#d make separte columns for year, month, and DOY
phenocam <- phenocam %>%
  mutate(year = year(datetime),
         month = month(datetime),
         DOY = yday(datetime))
phenocam

#e calculate mean NVDI per month per year
phenocam %>%
  group_by(year, month) %>%
  summarise(mean_NVDI = mean(NDVI_c, na.rm = TRUE))

```


### 5. Vectors (10 pts)

```{r}
lyrics <- c("This ain't Texas (woo),",
           "ain't no hold 'em (hey)/",
           "So lay your cards down, down, down, down")
```


```{r}
print("2a:")
str_length(lyrics)

print("2b:")
str_detect(lyrics, "Texas")

print("2c:")
str_count(lyrics, "down")

print("2d:")
str_extract(lyrics, "\\((.*?)\\)")
```

### 6. Dugout Data (15 pts)


### 4. NDVI from the Santa Rita Experimental Range (20 pts)

```{r}
phenocam <- read_csv("neon_srer_ndvi_phencom.csv", skip = 17, col_names = TRUE,
                    col_types = "ccncc") %>%
  select(date, local_std_time, contains("mean"), NDVI_c)
```

```{r}
#a create datetime column using unite

  unite("datetime", date, local_std_time, sep = " ")
phenocam

  mutate(datetime = ymd_hms(datetime))
phenocam

  mutate(year = year(datetime),
         month = month(datetime),
         DOY = yday(datetime))

  group_by(year, month) %>%
  summarise(mean_NVDI = mean(NDVI_c, na.rm = TRUE))
```

---
title: "Assignment 9 Answer Key"
author: "Ellen Bledsoe"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format: pdf
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

### 1. Working with Raster Data in `terra` (20 pts)


```{r answers-1}
# 1a
# When loading terra library, will print version number to console
library(terra)

# 1b
# Read in data with rast
prec <- rast("image_files/global_precipitation.tif")
# Print prec raster to see EPSG (4326)
prec

# 1c
# Geographic extent of Himalayas, roughly, is (lat, lon)
# Top left: 40.37, 69.33
# Bottom right: 26.93, 97.15
# ext takes arguments in this order: xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax
# or, in human: left, right, bottom, top
hima_ext <- ext(c(69.33, 97.15, 26.93, 40.37))

# 1d
# Crop precipitation to Himalayan extent
prec_hima <- crop(prec, hima_ext)
# Print new raster to see extent
prec_hima
```

### 2. Printing Maps (20 points)


```{r answers-2}
# 2a
# Plot the cropped precipitation data
plot(prec_hima)

# 2b
# Plot the same data, this time adding a title
plot(prec_hima, main = "Mean annual precipitation (mm)")

# 2c
# Define the palette first, using the topo.colors function
prec_cols <- topo.colors(n = 50)
# Plot the map with those new colors
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)

# 2d
# Define palette with hcl.colors, several options from Color Brewer, example
# below uses Yellow-Green-Blue palette, reversed so blue is highest
prec_cols <- rev(hcl.colors(n = 50, palette = "YlGnBu"))
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)
```

### 3. Working with Vector Data in `terra` (20 points)


```{r answer-3}
# 3a
# Create data frame for three peaks
peaks <- data.frame(peak = c("K2", "Annapurna", "Mount Everest"),
                    lat = c(35.93, 28.53, 28.05),
                    lon = c(76.50, 83.81, 86.91))

# 3b
# Convert data frame to SpatVector object
peaks_vect <- vect(peaks)

# 3c
# Draw base precipitation plot
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)
plot(peaks_vect, add = TRUE)

# 3d
# Add lines with lines function
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)
plot(peaks_vect, add = TRUE)
text(peaks_vect, labels = "peak", pos = 4)
```

### 4. Modifying Raster Values (20 points)


```{r answer-4}
# 4a
# Load the data
elev <- rast("image_files/global_elevation.tif")
# Print the object to see range of elevations, safe to infer units are meters
elev

# 4b
# 1 foot = 3.28084 meters
elev <- elev * 3.28084

# 4c
# Crop to same geographic extent as for precipitation data
elev_hima <- crop(elev, hima_ext)

# 4d
plot(elev_hima)
```

### 5. Converting Raster Objects to Spatial Vector Objects (20 points)

```{r answer-5}
# 5a
# Define elevation threshold
alpine_min <- 13000

# 5b
# Create logical raster of high elevation (TRUE/FALSE)
elev_alpine <- elev_hima >= alpine_min

# 5c
# Convert all TRUE values to NA (want the polygon to shade out areas of low
# elevation, which are FALSE in this case)
elev_alpine[elev_alpine] <- NA

# 5d
# Convert the raster with FALSE values only at places below 13,000 feet
low_elev_poly <- as.polygons(elev_alpine)

# 5e
# Plot the precipitation map
plot(prec_hima, col = prec_cols)
# Add black polygon with 50% transparency to shade out low-elevation sites
polys(x = low_elev_poly, col = "black", border = NA, alpha = 0.5)
```

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/ellenbledsoe/Documents/Git/DataWrangling/Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wants to create a Quarto website from course material in the DataWrangling repo, published via GitHub Pages using a `docs/` folder. Specific requirements refined through conversation:
   - Include student-facing lessons and assignments only (no instructor versions, no answer key files in nav)
   - Add a "View Answer Key" button to each assignment page (HTML-only, invisible in PDF)
   - Answer key pages should show rendered outputs but hide code (`echo: false` — already set in originals)
   - Convert .Rmd lesson/assignment files to .qmd (keeping original format settings)
   - Structure by topic name (not week numbers), maintaining curriculum order
   - Use newest file version when duplicates exist (e.g., `Week7_` files in `Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/`)
   - Site title: "Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists"; course description uses "RNR 437/537" (not WFSC 496B/596B)
   - Include Week01_Spreadsheets with placeholder `Week1_Spreadsheets.qmd` (user will add file manually)
   - Theme: cosmo; Navigation: left sidebar by topic
   - Architecture: same repo, Quarto website project (Option C) — existing .qmd files untouched, project-level HTML rendering overrides document-level `format: pdf`
   - For the Reproducibility assignment answer key button, link to Week 7 Solving Bigger Problems answer key (`../../Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`)
   - All work on a new branch: `quarto-website`
   - GitHub Pages setup: deploy from `main` branch, `/docs` folder (manual step after merge)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Quarto website project (`type: website`) with `output-dir: docs`
   - Quarto conditional content: `::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}` — renders only in HTML, not PDF/GFM
   - Quarto website projects render all listed files as HTML regardless of document-level `format: pdf`
   - `.Rmd` → `.qmd` conversion: change `output: pdf_document` to `format: pdf`, keep all other content identical
   - Answer key files use `knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)` in setup chunk to hide code
   - GitHub Pages via `docs/` folder on `main` branch
   - Quarto 1.9.36 (updated from 1.5.55 via `brew install --cask quarto`)
   - cosmo Bootstrap theme for Quarto HTML

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`_quarto.yml`** (created at repo root)
     - Defines Quarto website project with `output-dir: docs`, cosmo theme, docked sidebar with 15 topic sections in curriculum order
     - Key structure:
     ```yaml
     project:
       type: website
       output-dir: docs
     website:
       title: "Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists"
       sidebar:
         style: docked
         search: true
         contents:
           - section: "Spreadsheets"
             contents:
               - href: Week01_Spreadsheets/Week1_Spreadsheets.qmd
                 text: Spreadsheets
           - section: "Introduction to R"
             ...
     format:
       html:
         theme: cosmo
         toc: true
     ```

   - **`index.qmd`** (created at repo root)
     - Homepage listing "RNR 437/537: Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists" and 15 topics

   - **`Week06_TidyData/Week6_TidyData.qmd`** (created — converted from .Rmd)
     - Full lesson on making untidy data tidy; YAML changed from `output: pdf_document` to `format: pdf`

   - **`Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.qmd`** (created — converted from .Rmd, button added)
     - Answer key button inserted after due date line:
     ```markdown
     ::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
     [View Answer Key](Week6_Assignment_AnswerKey.html){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
     :::
     ```

   - **`Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_VersionControl.qmd`** (created — converted from .Rmd)
     - Version control lesson

   - **`Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_GeospatialData.qmd`** (created — converted from .Rmd)
     - Geospatial data lesson by Jeff Oliver

   - **12 existing assignment .qmd files** (edited — button inserted after Due Date, before first exercise heading):
     - `Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week3_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week4_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week5_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Assignment.qmd` → links to `../../Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week10_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week11_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week12_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week13_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`
     - `Week14_GeospatialData/Week9_Assignment.qmd` → links to `Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`

   - **11 answer key .qmd files created** (converted from .Rmd, only YAML change: `output: pdf_document` → `format: pdf`):
     - `Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week07_DatesandStrings/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - `Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`
     - Note: `Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` already existed as .qmd — no conversion needed

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Quarto version too old (1.5.55)**: User ran `brew install --cask quarto` to upgrade to 1.9.36. Initial brew attempt failed because it needed sudo in an interactive terminal; user ran it directly in Terminal.
   - **Background agent denied Edit tool permission**: Agent tasked with adding buttons to 12 assignment files was denied the Edit tool. Fixed by reading all assignment files directly and performing all 12 edits manually in one batch.
   - **Background agent denied Write tool permission and hit usage limit**: Agent tasked with converting 11 answer key .Rmd files to .qmd was denied Write tool and then hit usage limit. Fixed by reading all 11 files in one parallel batch and writing all 11 .qmd files directly.
   - **User corrected answer key approach**: Original plan called for creating new answer key files with `echo: false` added. User clarified that `echo: false` is already present in the originals — just convert .Rmd to .qmd without adding anything.
   - **User corrected Reproducibility answer key**: Original plan had a `Week8_Assignment_key_web.qmd`. User specified to use the Week 7 Solving Bigger Problems answer key file instead, pointing to `../../Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html`.
   - **Course name correction**: Original plan used WFSC 496B/596B. User corrected to RNR 437/537 (for the description/body only; website title stays as-is).
   - **Missing Spreadsheets week**: User noted Week01_Spreadsheets was missing from the plan. Added as first section in sidebar with placeholder path `Week01_Spreadsheets/Week1_Spreadsheets.qmd`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Architecture decision**: Chose Option C (same repo, Quarto website project) over separate repo with submodule or GitHub Actions sync. Key insight: Quarto website projects render HTML regardless of document-level `format: pdf`, so existing files need no modification.
   - **Answer key button visibility**: Used `::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}` Quarto div so buttons appear only in HTML renders, leaving PDF/GFM output completely unchanged.
   - **Duplicate files across weeks**: Used newest .qmd versions (e.g., `Week14_DatesandTimes.qmd` not `Week7_DatesandTimes.Rmd`; `Week7_SolvingBiggerProblems.qmd` not `Week8_SolvingBiggerProblems.Rmd`).
   - **Week09_Assignment.qmd in Week14 folder**: Treated as the current geospatial assignment (actively modified per git status).

6. All user messages:
   - "I want to create a website using the material in this repo. I want to make it with quarto and github pages via a docs folder"
   - "The user wants to clarify these questions." (rejected tool use for clarifying questions)
   - "I want to include the lessons and assignments, but not the instructor versions or keys. For the assignment pages, please add a button that points to the assignment 'AnswerKey' quarto file. This should produce the expected outputs for the assignment without showing the code used to make it. I still have some more instructions"
   - "There are some folders which have .rmd files instead of quarto files. Please convert those. Also, structure the content without reference to weeks (focus on the content). Keep the content in the same order. There are some weeks that are a little confusing--use the newest material (e.g., week7 soving bigger problems instead of week 8 solving bigger problems)."
   - "ok yes, lets go with c" (approving Option C same-repo architecture)
   - "You shouldn't need to create new answer key files; echo = false is already there. Also, for the reproducibility answer key, us the week 7 'solving bigger problems' answer key file"
   - (Approved plan via ExitPlanMode)
   - "Don't forget the week on Spreadsheets! i'll be manually adding files later"

   - "The course name is now RNR 437/537 instead of WFSC 496B/596B"
   - "Leave the website title as is, but fix the name in the description"
   - "let's resume" (after agent hit usage limit)
   - Summary request message (current)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Run `quarto render` from repo root to build the site into `docs/` and verify it works correctly

input.plan:
# Plan: Quarto Website for Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists

## Context
The repo contains course material for WFSC 496B/596B. The goal is to build a Quarto website published via a `docs/` folder on GitHub Pages, **within the same repo**. A Quarto website project renders all listed files as HTML regardless of document-level `format: pdf` settings — so existing lesson/assignment files are untouched and continue to produce PDF/GFM when rendered individually. Only new files are created, plus minimal conditional additions to assignment files that don't affect non-HTML output.

---

## Architecture (Option C: Same repo, Quarto website project)

- `_quarto.yml` at repo root defines the website project with `output-dir: docs`
- Existing `.qmd` lesson/assignment files: **not modified** (Quarto website overrides their format to HTML automatically)
- `.Rmd` lesson/assignment files without `.qmd` equivalents: **converted to `.qmd`** (keeping original format settings — PDF output unchanged)
- Answer key files: **new `.qmd` files created** with `execute: echo: false` (originals untouched)
- Assignment files: receive a **conditional HTML button** using `::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}` — button is invisible in PDF/GFM renders

---

## Site Configuration

- **Theme:** cosmo
- **Navigation:** Left sidebar grouped by topic, in curriculum order
- **Output dir:** `docs/`

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 0: Create branch
```bash
git checkout -b quarto-website
```

### Step 1: Create `_quarto.yml` at repo root

```yaml
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs

website:
  title: "Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists"
  navbar:
    left:
      - text: Home
        href: index.qmd
  sidebar:
    style: docked
    search: true
    contents:
      - section: "Introduction to R"
        contents:
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_IntrotoR.qmd
            text: Introduction to R
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Vectors.qmd
            text: Vectors
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Data Tables"
        contents:
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_DataTables.qmd
            text: Data Tables
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_Pipes.qmd
            text: Pipes
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Groups and Joins"
        contents:
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Vectors_and_DataFames.qmd
            text: Vectors and Data Frames
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Aggregation.qmd
            text: Aggregation
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Binds_Joins.qmd
            text: Joins and Binds
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Data Visualization"
        contents:
          - href: Week05_DataViz/Week5_DataVisualization.qmd
            text: Data Visualization
          - href: Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Tidy Data"
        contents:
          - href: Week06_TidyData/Week6_TidyData.qmd
            text: Tidy Data
          - href: Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Dates and Strings"
        contents:
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_DatesandTimes.qmd
            text: Dates and Times
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Strings.qmd
            text: Strings
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Solving Bigger Problems"
        contents:
          - href: Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_SolvingBiggerProblems.qmd
            text: Solving Bigger Problems
          - href: Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Reproducibility"
        contents:
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Reproducibility.qmd
            text: Reproducibility
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/filepaths.qmd
            text: File Paths
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Version Control"
        contents:
          - href: Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_VersionControl.qmd
            text: Version Control
          - href: Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Functions"
        contents:
          - href: Week11_Functions/Week11_Functions.qmd
            text: Functions
          - href: Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Making Choices"
        contents:
          - href: Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_MakingChoices.qmd
            text: Making Choices
          - href: Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Iteration"
        contents:
          - href: Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Iteration.qmd
            text: Iteration
          - href: Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Geospatial Data"
        contents:
          - href: Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_GeospatialData.qmd
            text: Geospatial Data
          - href: Week14_GeospatialData/Week9_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment

format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
```

### Step 2: Create `index.qmd` at repo root
Simple homepage with course title, brief description, and a note to use the sidebar to navigate.

### Step 3: Convert `.Rmd` lesson + assignment files to `.qmd`
These files have no `.qmd` equivalent and are referenced by the website. Convert by copying content and renaming extension. **Keep all existing YAML fields unchanged** (including `format: pdf`) so individual rendering is unaffected.

file.content:
# Plan: Quarto Website for Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists

## Context
The repo contains course material for WFSC 496B/596B. The goal is to build a Quarto website published via a `docs/` folder on GitHub Pages, **within the same repo**. A Quarto website project renders all listed files as HTML regardless of document-level `format: pdf` settings — so existing lesson/assignment files are untouched and continue to produce PDF/GFM when rendered individually. Only new files are created, plus minimal conditional additions to assignment files that don't affect non-HTML output.

---

## Architecture (Option C: Same repo, Quarto website project)

- `_quarto.yml` at repo root defines the website project with `output-dir: docs`
- Existing `.qmd` lesson/assignment files: **not modified** (Quarto website overrides their format to HTML automatically)
- `.Rmd` lesson/assignment files without `.qmd` equivalents: **converted to `.qmd`** (keeping original format settings — PDF output unchanged)
- Answer key files: **new `.qmd` files created** with `execute: echo: false` (originals untouched)
- Assignment files: receive a **conditional HTML button** using `::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}` — button is invisible in PDF/GFM renders

---

## Site Configuration

- **Theme:** cosmo
- **Navigation:** Left sidebar grouped by topic, in curriculum order
- **Output dir:** `docs/`

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 0: Create branch
```bash
git checkout -b quarto-website
```

### Step 1: Create `_quarto.yml` at repo root

```yaml
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs

website:
  title: "Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists"
  navbar:
    left:
      - text: Home
        href: index.qmd
  sidebar:
    style: docked
    search: true
    contents:
      - section: "Introduction to R"
        contents:
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_IntrotoR.qmd
            text: Introduction to R
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Vectors.qmd
            text: Vectors
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Data Tables"
        contents:
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_DataTables.qmd
            text: Data Tables
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_Pipes.qmd
            text: Pipes
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Groups and Joins"
        contents:
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Vectors_and_DataFames.qmd
            text: Vectors and Data Frames
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Aggregation.qmd
            text: Aggregation
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Binds_Joins.qmd
            text: Joins and Binds
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Data Visualization"
        contents:
          - href: Week05_DataViz/Week5_DataVisualization.qmd
            text: Data Visualization
          - href: Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Tidy Data"
        contents:
          - href: Week06_TidyData/Week6_TidyData.qmd
            text: Tidy Data
          - href: Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Dates and Strings"
        contents:
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_DatesandTimes.qmd
            text: Dates and Times
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Strings.qmd
            text: Strings
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Solving Bigger Problems"
        contents:
          - href: Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_SolvingBiggerProblems.qmd
            text: Solving Bigger Problems
          - href: Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Reproducibility"
        contents:
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Reproducibility.qmd
            text: Reproducibility
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/filepaths.qmd
            text: File Paths
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Version Control"
        contents:
          - href: Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_VersionControl.qmd
            text: Version Control
          - href: Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Functions"
        contents:
          - href: Week11_Functions/Week11_Functions.qmd
            text: Functions
          - href: Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Making Choices"
        contents:
          - href: Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_MakingChoices.qmd
            text: Making Choices
          - href: Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Iteration"
        contents:
          - href: Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Iteration.qmd
            text: Iteration
          - href: Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Geospatial Data"
        contents:
          - href: Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_GeospatialData.qmd
            text: Geospatial Data
          - href: Week14_GeospatialData/Week9_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment

format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
```

### Step 2: Create `index.qmd` at repo root
Simple homepage with course title, brief description, and a note to use the sidebar to navigate.

### Step 3: Convert `.Rmd` lesson + assignment files to `.qmd`
These files have no `.qmd` equivalent and are referenced by the website. Convert by copying content and renaming extension. **Keep all existing YAML fields unchanged** (including `format: pdf`) so individual rendering is unaffected.

| Source (keep) | New file to create |
|---|---|
| `Week06_TidyData/Week6_TidyData.Rmd` | `Week06_TidyData/Week6_TidyData.qmd` |
| `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.Rmd` | `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.qmd` |
| `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_VersionControl.Rmd` | `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_VersionControl.qmd` |
| `Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_GeospatialData.Rmd` | `Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_GeospatialData.qmd` |

Notes:
- `Week14_GeospatialData/Week9_Assignment.qmd` already exists — use it as the geospatial assignment
- `Week11_Functions/Week11_Functions.qmd` already exists (confirmed in inventory)

### Step 4: Convert `.Rmd` answer key files to `.qmd`
Answer key files already have `execute: echo: false` set. For files that only exist as `.Rmd`, create `.qmd` equivalents (same content, no other changes). The `.Rmd` originals are kept as-is.

| New `.qmd` to create | Source `.Rmd` |
|---|---|
| `Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week3_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week4_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week5_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week6_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week07_DatesandStrings/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week10_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week11_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week12_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week13_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |

Already `.qmd` (no conversion needed):
- `Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`

These answer key files are **not listed in the sidebar** — they're render targets only, linked via buttons.

### Step 5: Add conditional answer key buttons to assignment `.qmd` files
Add this block near the top of each assignment file (after any intro paragraph). The `::: {.content-visible}` div **only renders in HTML** — PDF/GFM output is unchanged.

```markdown
::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
[View Answer Key](Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.html){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
:::
```

Button link mapping (relative paths within each directory):

| Assignment file | Button href |
|---|---|
| `Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment.qmd` | `Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment.qmd` | `Week3_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment.qmd` | `Week4_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment.qmd` | `Week5_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.qmd` | `Week6_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Assignment.qmd` | `Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment.qmd` | `Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Assignment.qmd` | `../../Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment.qmd` | `Week10_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment.qmd` | `Week11_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment.qmd` | `Week12_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment.qmd` | `Week13_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week14_GeospatialData/Week9_Assignment.qmd` | `Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |

---

## GitHub Pages Setup (manual step for user after merge)
1. Merge the `quarto-website` branch to `main`
2. Run `quarto render` from the repo root to populate `docs/`
3. Go to repo Settings → Pages → set source to branch `main`, folder `/docs`

---

## Verification
- Run `quarto render` from repo root — should output to `docs/`
- Open `docs/index.html` in browser
- Verify sidebar shows all topics in order, without week numbers
- Click an assignment → confirm answer key button appears
- Click the button → confirm answer key page shows rendered output but no code
- Render a lesson `.qmd` individually (e.g., `quarto render Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_IntrotoR.qmd`) → confirm it still produces a PDF
- Push branch to GitHub, open PR

# Plan: Quarto Website for Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists

## Context
The repo contains course material for WFSC 496B/596B. The goal is to build a Quarto website published via a `docs/` folder on GitHub Pages, **within the same repo**. A Quarto website project renders all listed files as HTML regardless of document-level `format: pdf` settings — so existing lesson/assignment files are untouched and continue to produce PDF/GFM when rendered individually. Only new files are created, plus minimal conditional additions to assignment files that don't affect non-HTML output.

---

## Architecture (Option C: Same repo, Quarto website project)

- `_quarto.yml` at repo root defines the website project with `output-dir: docs`
- Existing `.qmd` lesson/assignment files: **not modified** (Quarto website overrides their format to HTML automatically)
- `.Rmd` lesson/assignment files without `.qmd` equivalents: **converted to `.qmd`** (keeping original format settings — PDF output unchanged)
- Answer key files: **new `.qmd` files created** with `execute: echo: false` (originals untouched)
- Assignment files: receive a **conditional HTML button** using `::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}` — button is invisible in PDF/GFM renders

---

## Site Configuration

- **Theme:** cosmo
- **Navigation:** Left sidebar grouped by topic, in curriculum order
- **Output dir:** `docs/`

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 0: Create branch
```bash
git checkout -b quarto-website
```

### Step 1: Create `_quarto.yml` at repo root

```yaml
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs

website:
  title: "Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists"
  navbar:
    left:
      - text: Home
        href: index.qmd
  sidebar:
    style: docked
    search: true
    contents:
      - section: "Introduction to R"
        contents:
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_IntrotoR.qmd
            text: Introduction to R
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Vectors.qmd
            text: Vectors
          - href: Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Data Tables"
        contents:
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_DataTables.qmd
            text: Data Tables
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_Pipes.qmd
            text: Pipes
          - href: Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Groups and Joins"
        contents:
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Vectors_and_DataFames.qmd
            text: Vectors and Data Frames
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Aggregation.qmd
            text: Aggregation
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Binds_Joins.qmd
            text: Joins and Binds
          - href: Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Data Visualization"
        contents:
          - href: Week05_DataViz/Week5_DataVisualization.qmd
            text: Data Visualization
          - href: Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Tidy Data"
        contents:
          - href: Week06_TidyData/Week6_TidyData.qmd
            text: Tidy Data
          - href: Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Dates and Strings"
        contents:
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_DatesandTimes.qmd
            text: Dates and Times
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Strings.qmd
            text: Strings
          - href: Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Solving Bigger Problems"
        contents:
          - href: Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_SolvingBiggerProblems.qmd
            text: Solving Bigger Problems
          - href: Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Reproducibility"
        contents:
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Reproducibility.qmd
            text: Reproducibility
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/filepaths.qmd
            text: File Paths
          - href: Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Version Control"
        contents:
          - href: Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_VersionControl.qmd
            text: Version Control
          - href: Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Functions"
        contents:
          - href: Week11_Functions/Week11_Functions.qmd
            text: Functions
          - href: Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Making Choices"
        contents:
          - href: Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_MakingChoices.qmd
            text: Making Choices
          - href: Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Iteration"
        contents:
          - href: Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Iteration.qmd
            text: Iteration
          - href: Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment
      - section: "Geospatial Data"
        contents:
          - href: Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_GeospatialData.qmd
            text: Geospatial Data
          - href: Week14_GeospatialData/Week9_Assignment.qmd
            text: Assignment

format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
```

### Step 2: Create `index.qmd` at repo root
Simple homepage with course title, brief description, and a note to use the sidebar to navigate.

### Step 3: Convert `.Rmd` lesson + assignment files to `.qmd`
These files have no `.qmd` equivalent and are referenced by the website. Convert by copying content and renaming extension. **Keep all existing YAML fields unchanged** (including `format: pdf`) so individual rendering is unaffected.

| Source (keep) | New file to create |
|---|---|
| `Week06_TidyData/Week6_TidyData.Rmd` | `Week06_TidyData/Week6_TidyData.qmd` |
| `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.Rmd` | `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.qmd` |
| `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_VersionControl.Rmd` | `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_VersionControl.qmd` |
| `Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_GeospatialData.Rmd` | `Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_GeospatialData.qmd` |

Notes:
- `Week14_GeospatialData/Week9_Assignment.qmd` already exists — use it as the geospatial assignment
- `Week11_Functions/Week11_Functions.qmd` already exists (confirmed in inventory)

### Step 4: Convert `.Rmd` answer key files to `.qmd`
Answer key files already have `execute: echo: false` set. For files that only exist as `.Rmd`, create `.qmd` equivalents (same content, no other changes). The `.Rmd` originals are kept as-is.

| New `.qmd` to create | Source `.Rmd` |
|---|---|
| `Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week3_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week4_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week5_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week6_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week07_DatesandStrings/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week10_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week11_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week12_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week13_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |
| `Week14_GeospatialData/Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd` | `Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.Rmd` |

Already `.qmd` (no conversion needed):
- `Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.qmd`

These answer key files are **not listed in the sidebar** — they're render targets only, linked via buttons.

### Step 5: Add conditional answer key buttons to assignment `.qmd` files
Add this block near the top of each assignment file (after any intro paragraph). The `::: {.content-visible}` div **only renders in HTML** — PDF/GFM output is unchanged.

```markdown
::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
[View Answer Key](Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.html){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
:::
```

Button link mapping (relative paths within each directory):

| Assignment file | Button href |
|---|---|
| `Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_Assignment.qmd` | `Week2_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week03_DataTables/Week3_Assignment.qmd` | `Week3_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week04_Groups_and_Joins/Week4_Assignment.qmd` | `Week4_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week05_DataViz/Week5_Assignment.qmd` | `Week5_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week06_TidyData/Week6_Assignment.qmd` | `Week6_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week07_DatesandStrings/Week14_Assignment.qmd` | `Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment.qmd` | `Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week08_Reproducibility/scripts/Week8_Assignment.qmd` | `../../Week08_SolvingBiggerProblems/Week7_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week10_VersionControl/code/Week10_Assignment.qmd` | `Week10_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week11_Functions/Week11_Assignment.qmd` | `Week11_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week12_MakingChoices/Week12_Assignment.qmd` | `Week12_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week13_Iteration/scripts/Week13_Assignment.qmd` | `Week13_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |
| `Week14_GeospatialData/Week9_Assignment.qmd` | `Week14_Assignment_AnswerKey.html` |

---

## GitHub Pages Setup (manual step for user after merge)
1. Merge the `quarto-website` branch to `main`
2. Run `quarto render` from the repo root to populate `docs/`
3. Go to repo Settings → Pages → set source to branch `main`, folder `/docs`

---

## Verification
- Run `quarto render` from repo root — should output to `docs/`
- Open `docs/index.html` in browser
- Verify sidebar shows all topics in order, without week numbers

- Render a lesson `.qmd` individually (e.g., `quarto render Week02_IntrotoR/Week2_IntrotoR.qmd`) → confirm it still produces a PDF
- Push branch to GitHub, open PR

# Plan: Quarto Website for Data Wrangling in R for Ecologists

## Context
The repo contains course material for WFSC 496B/596B. The goal is to build a Quarto website published via a `docs/` folder on GitHub Pages, **within the same repo**. A Quarto website project renders all listed files as HTML regardless of document-level `format: pdf` settings — so existing lesson/assignment files are untouched and continue to produce PDF/GFM when rendered individually. Only new files are created, plus minimal conditional additions to assignment files that don't affect non-HTML output.

---

## Architecture (Option C: Same repo, Quarto website project)

- `_quarto.yml` at repo root defines the website project with `output-dir: docs`
- Existing `.qmd` lesson/assignment files: **not modified** (Quarto website overrides their format to HTML automatically)
- `.Rmd` lesson/assignment files without `.qmd` equivalents: **converted to `.qmd`** (keeping original format settings — PDF output unchanged)
- Answer key files: **new `.qmd` files created** with `execute: echo: false` (originals untouched)
- Assignment files: receive a **conditional HTML button** using `::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}` — button is invisible in PDF/GFM renders

---

## Site Configuration

- **Theme:** cosmo
- **Navigation:** Left sidebar grouped by topic, in curriculum order
- **Output dir:** `docs/`

---
